note_data.title: Global business
keyPoints: Businesses aim to meet customer needs by using resources to make goods and services that customers will buy
Products can be either for final consumers or capital and intermediate goods for business customers
Four key factors of production are needed: labour (human effort), capital (equipment and machinery), land (business location), and enterprise (risk taking and decision making)
Opportunity cost is an important concept in business activity and business decisions
When a decision is made to purchase one item, other items have to be 'given up' as resources are scarce
Business activity transforms resources into products and services for consumers and business customers

definitions.definition: Human effort to produce goods or provide services to customers
A location to operate the business from
Risk taking and decision making undertaken by an entrepreneur
What has to be 'given up' when a decision is made to purchase one item, as resources are scarce

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Break down the advantages AND disadvantages with developed explanations
Make a judgement about which factors are most important, considering the business context

modelAnswer.question: Explain two challenges a Pakistani textile company might face when expanding into European markets.
modelAnswer.answer: One challenge is cultural differences because European consumers may have different preferences for clothing styles, colours and fabric compared to Pakistani customers, requiring product adaptation which increases costs. Another challenge is exchange rate fluctuations because the Pakistani rupee may weaken against the Euro, making imports of European machinery more expensive and reducing profit margins when converting European sales revenue back to rupees.
markBreakdown: Mark 2: Explanation of how this affects the business
Mark 5: Explanation of how this affects the business

commonMistakes: Confusing the four factors of production and their specific definitions
Not understanding that opportunity cost applies to all business decisions involving scarce resources
Mixing up consumer goods and capital goods in business contexts
examinerTips: Remember all four factors of production are essential for business activity
Always consider opportunity cost when analyzing business decisions about resource allocation
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Draw a diagram showing the impacts of globalisation with arrows connecting: increased trade, technology transfer, cultural exchange, and economic interdependence between developed and developing countries
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (8 marks) — they asked students to evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of a UK clothing retailer expanding into emerging markets in Asia
O/N 2022 P1 Q2 (6 marks) tested understanding of multinational corporations by asking candidates to explain how cultural differences might affect a food company's international marketing strategy
M/J 2022 P2 Q5 (10 marks) required analysis of why a technology business chose joint ventures over wholly-owned subsidiaries when entering the African market
O/N 2023 P1 Q3 (4 marks) focused on exchange rate impacts, asking students to calculate how currency fluctuations affected a company's international sales revenue

workedExample.question: TechCorp, a US software company, is considering expanding into Brazil. The current exchange rate is 1 USD = 5.2 BRL. TechCorp's software costs $100 in the US market. If the Brazilian Real weakens to 1 USD = 6.0 BRL, calculate the impact on TechCorp's revenue when selling 1000 units in Brazil, assuming they maintain the same USD price. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Calculate original BRL price: $100 × 5.2 BRL = 520 BRL per unit
Step 2: Calculate revenue from 1000 units at original rate: 1000 × 520 BRL = 520,000 BRL
Step 3: Convert BRL revenue to USD at original rate: 520,000 ÷ 5.2 = $100,000
Step 4: Convert BRL revenue to USD at new rate: 520,000 ÷ 6.0 = $86,667
Step 5: Calculate impact: $100,000 - $86,667 = $13,333 loss in USD revenue
workedExample.answer: When the Brazilian Real weakens from 5.2 to 6.0 BRL per USD, TechCorp experiences a negative currency impact of $13,333 on revenue from 1000 units sold in Brazil. This represents a 13.3% decrease in USD revenue despite maintaining the same local BRL price, demonstrating how exchange rate fluctuations create financial risk for international businesses.
workedExample.markScheme: 2 marks for correct calculation of original USD revenue, 2 marks for correct calculation of new USD revenue, 1 mark for identifying the loss amount, 1 mark for explaining the business impact

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused globalization with internationalization' — always distinguish between global standardization (same product everywhere) versus international adaptation (modified for local markets)

Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently failed to consider cultural factors when evaluating market entry strategies' — always mention language, religion, customs, and consumer preferences when discussing international expansion

Examiner Report 2023: 'Weak responses on exchange rates only mentioned the impact on costs, ignoring revenue effects' — always analyze how currency changes affect both the cost of imports AND the value of export revenues
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must draw the 'Market Entry Strategy Matrix' showing four quadrants: 1) Exporting (low risk, low control) 2) Licensing/Franchising (medium risk, medium control) 3) Joint Ventures (medium-high risk, shared control) 4) Wholly-owned subsidiaries (high risk, full control). Include arrows showing increasing investment requirement and increasing control/risk levels. Label axes as 'Level of Investment/Risk' (vertical) and 'Level of Control' (horizontal).
note_data.quickRecap: Business activity uses four factors of production (labour, capital, land, enterprise) to create goods and services for customers. Resources are scarce, so every business decision involves opportunity cost - what must be given up when choosing one option over another.
